definition: A state of not knowing what to decide; a state of difficulty or perplexity; a state of uncertainty, hesitation or puzzlement.
example: In the paragraph analysis, _______ to frame the main argument clearly in that section. as it is close to dilemma.

definition: Inactive, quiet, at rest.
example: The writer's position seemed _______ to explain the shift in viewpoint for that question. as it is close to dormant.

definition: A recondite (hidden or obscure) person or thing.
example: To support the argument, the author had to _______ before evaluating the counterpoint for a stronger interpretation. to explain the idea of a recondite (hidden or obscure) person or thing.

definition: Fragrant or aromatic; having a sweet scent.
example: The GRE passage introduced a _______ according to the evidence provided within the discussion. where a similar notion is aromatic.

definition: Exile, banish, remove, or send away.
example: While analyzing the claim, students needed to _______ so that the logic stayed consistent in the GRE passage. with focus on exile, banish, remove, or send away.

definition: To object; to express disapproval (with, against).
example: Before choosing an option, candidates tried to _______ to make the conclusion more convincing in the critical reading set. where a similar notion is demur.

definition: To reject the truth or validity of; to deny.
example: In the final paragraph, the reviewer decided to _______ to make the conclusion more convincing in the critical reading set. where a similar notion is contradict.

definition: An outwardly projecting part of a fortification, trench system, or line of defense.
example: In the model answer, the tone felt _______ throughout the argument analysis in the reading section. to explain the idea of an outwardly projecting part of a fortification, trench.

definition: Scornfully mocking or cynical.
example: The critic described the perspective as _______ throughout the argument analysis in the reading section. to explain the idea of scornfully mocking or cynical.

definition: (of a person) Given to vulgar verbal abuse; foul-mouthed.
example: In the model answer, the tone felt _______ when compared with standard theories in the text. with focus on (of a person) given to vulgar verbal abuse;.

definition: Sequestration; separation
example: While analyzing the claim, students needed to _______ so that the logic stayed consistent in the GRE passage. with focus on sequestration; separation.

definition: Disposed to solicit; eager to obtain something desirable, or to avoid anything evil.
example: The writer's position seemed _______ according to the evidence provided within the discussion. where a similar notion is attentive.

definition: Cunning, sometimes manifested as trickery.
example: The central idea was clarified by _______ which highlighted the author's intent for the passage. with focus on cunning, sometimes manifested as trickery.

definition: False, not authentic, not genuine.
example: In the model answer, the tone felt _______ throughout the argument analysis in the reading section. to explain the idea of false, not authentic, not genuine.

definition: Silent; temperamentally untalkative; disinclined to speak.
example: The GRE passage introduced a _______ to explain the shift in viewpoint for that question. as it is close to reticent.

definition: Clinging to an object or surface; adhesive.
example: The critic described the perspective as _______ throughout the argument analysis in the reading section. to explain the idea of clinging to an object or surface; adhesive.

bangla: একটি দীর্ঘ, রাগান্বিত বা হিংসাত্মক বক্তৃতা
definition: A long, angry or violent speech; a diatribe.
example: Before choosing an option, candidates tried to _______ without weakening the central thesis during the reasoning task. as it is close to laisse.

bangla: একজন দালাল যে ব্যক্তিগত সুবিধা পেতে অন্যদের চাটুকার করে বা একজন আপত্তিকর দালাল বা
definition: A sycophant who flatters others to gain personal advantage or an obsequious lackey or minion
example: Before choosing an option, candidates tried to _______ without weakening the central thesis during the reasoning task. as it is close to sycophant.

definition: An absurd or grotesque misrepresentation.
example: To support the argument, the author had to _______ so that the logic stayed consistent in the GRE passage. with focus on an absurd or grotesque misrepresentation.

definition: Fitted to trench or cut; gutting; sharp.
example: The critic described the perspective as _______ throughout the argument analysis in the reading section. to explain the idea of fitted to trench or cut; gutting; sharp.
